description: Identifies potentially unnecessary Enhanced Fan-Out, shard overcapacity, retention overuse, and billing mode mismatches while protecting throughput and latency requirements.

cost.pricing_source: Static fallback estimate. Prefer AWS Pricing MCP, AWS Pricing API, or cost_report region-specific pricing when available.
cost.note: Kinesis Data Streams pricing varies by region and stream mode. Separate shard/stream hours, ingest, retrieval, EFO, retention, and on-demand/provisioned mode effects.

severity_rules.name: enhanced_fan_out_review_candidate
severity_rules.condition: enhanced_fan_out == true AND processing_interval_minutes >= processing_interval_minutes_batch_min AND no low_latency_sla AND no read_throughput_contention AND consumer_count <= standard_polling_safe_consumer_count
severity_rules.description: EFO may be unnecessary for batch or latency-tolerant consumers. Disable only after validating latency SLA, read throughput, consumer isolation, and iterator age in a pilot.

severity_rules.name: provisioned_shard_overcapacity
severity_rules.condition: stream_mode == PROVISIONED AND write_utilization_p95 < shard_write_utilization_p95_max_for_reduction_pct AND read_utilization_p95 < shard_read_utilization_p95_max_for_reduction_pct AND write_throttles == 0 AND read_throttles == 0 AND iterator_age_ms_p95 < iterator_age_ms_review_threshold
severity_rules.description: Shard capacity appears over-provisioned with safe p95 utilization, no throttling, and acceptable iterator age. Preserve peak headroom and account for partition-key skew before resharding.

severity_rules.name: billing_mode_model_candidate
severity_rules.condition: observed_traffic_pattern suggests alternative stream mode may be cheaper
severity_rules.description: Provisioned vs on-demand economics depend on traffic shape, region, throughput, and stream count. Model both modes before changing.

severity_rules.name: extended_retention_review
severity_rules.description: Extended retention may add cost. Reduce only when replay, audit, and recovery requirements are satisfied.

severity_rules.name: performance_risk_blocks_downsize
severity_rules.condition: write_throttles > 0 OR read_throttles > 0 OR iterator_age_ms_p95 >= iterator_age_ms_review_threshold OR consumer_lag_high == true
severity_rules.description: Throughput or latency risk exists. Do not reduce shards or disable EFO until throttling, iterator age, and consumer lag are resolved.

efo_justification_criteria.note: EFO can be justified when any strong business or technical requirement exists.
criteria: sub-second or strict low-latency consumer SLA
multiple consumers require dedicated read throughput per shard
critical consumer needs isolation from other consumers
standard GetRecords polling would cause read throughput contention
consumer architecture requires push-style SubscribeToShard behavior

validation_before_change: Check iterator age and consumer lag p95/max.
Check p95 and peak read/write throughput against shard limits.
Confirm latency SLA and consumer isolation requirements before disabling EFO.
Pilot changes and monitor rollback signals.
reporting: Separate EFO, shard-hour, retention, ingest, retrieval, and billing-mode savings.
Report confidence based on metrics coverage and SLA evidence.
